name: BCAA
summary: Human evidence for BCAA benefits is inconsistent and often inferior to complete protein or EAA contexts; authority status is insufficient for broad claims.
description: BCAA claims are inconsistent across studies and typically less robust than total protein or EAA approaches; benefits may depend on baseline diet, age, and training.
safetyNotes: Generally tolerated; caution maple syrup urine disease and severe liver disease.
contraindications: Avoid in maple syrup urine disease (inherited BCAA-metabolism disorder)

a placebo-controlled ALS trial was halted early after excess mortality in the BCAA-treated arm (Italian ALS Study Group 1993) so BCAA supplementation is not recommended for ALS outside clinician-directed care
